author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Pydantic ValidationError from field validators raises ValueError internally, which Pydantic wraps with context like 'type=value_error, input_value=...' — the str(error) produces the full technical dump. Must call error.errors() and extract the 'msg' field to get just the human-readable message.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: _extract_validation_messages() strips the 'Value error, ' prefix that Pydantic prepends to validator-raised ValueError messages. Without this strip, you get 'Value error, CPF inválido' instead of 'CPF inválido'.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: All 7 Telegram bot error handlers in handlers.py route through sanitize_error_for_user(), so this fix applies to patient creation, visit creation, PDF, NFSe, and all other flows.

FACT: cognitest auth.py proxy pattern is correct as-is: HTTPException for fixed-status errors (502/400), JSONResponse only for pass-through where status comes from upstream response.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Kept UUID v7 primary keys throughout cognitest despite pre-production status. Evaluated migration to integer PKs and decided against it for these reasons: (1) session_id, payment_id, result_id, organization_id all appear in public URL paths — sequential integers enable enumeration attacks across orgs; (2) organization_id is a FK in nearly every table, migrating it requires touching the entire schema; (3) UUID v7 is time-ordered so B-tree fragmentation (the main UUID perf concern) does not apply; (4) storage overhead (16 vs 4-8 bytes/row) is negligible at medical platform scale; (5) purely internal tables (commissions, payouts, notification_log, etc.) have no URL exposure so no benefit from integers either. Conclusion: UUID v7 throughout is the correct architecture for a multi-tenant medical platform with external-facing identifiers.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: PostgreSQL 18.1 has native uuidv7() function — no pg_uuidv7 extension needed. Confirmed working on production db (10.10.0.43:6432).

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ~10,700 total rows across 33 tables. 62 FK relationships. Self-referential FKs on: invoices.substitute_invoice_id, nfse_issuances.substitutes_issuance_id, pdf_template_fields.anchor_field_id.

The CogniTestRegisterRequest schema in MedSimples uses SexEnum directly. CogniTest RegisterRequest now constrains sex to Literal['male', 'female', 'not_informed'] to match exactly.

Matches the pattern of all other cognitest service-to-service endpoints (POST with org_id in body for RLS). payment_reference (Stripe payment_intent_id) uniquely identifies the InvoicePayment created by record-payment, so no separate payment_id needs to be stored on CogniTestPayment.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: exec() with __builtins__: {} (empty dict) blocks import, open, eval, exec at the name lookup level. Empty dict is safer than None — None allows C-level builtins to leak through in some Python versions.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: LLM tool errors are indirect prompt injection vectors (OWASP LLM Top 10 #2). All exceptions from namespace functions must go through sanitize_error_for_user() before being returned. See medsimples-0wz4.4.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: user_id is baked into each closure via lambda default capture. Do not use late-binding (lambda: medical_service.fn(user_id)) — use lambda user_id=user_id: to capture at definition time.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Function signatures in the prompt must exactly match the namespace in code_executor.py. If they diverge, the LLM will call functions with wrong signatures. Keep them in sync — consider auto-generating the prompt block from the namespace dict.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Lower temperature (0.3) for code generation vs conversation (0.7). Set this in _process_with_code_mode(), not in the prompt.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: generate() not generate_with_tools() — code-mode uses the simpler path. The agentic loop (up to 5 iterations) is replaced by a single LLM call that produces a complete code block.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: PDF detection — generate_visit_summary_pdf() returns a dict with 'pdf_path' key. Replicate the detection from ollama_client.py line 242 to populate generated_files in the response.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: __builtins__: {} is bypassed by ().__class__.__mro__[-1].__subclasses__() — standard Python 3 sandbox escape. AST validation blocking ast.Attribute nodes with __subclasses__, __mro__, __class__ is the correct mitigation. Source: HackTricks Python sandbox bypass, CVE-2025-22153.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ThreadPoolExecutor.result(timeout=N) does NOT kill the thread. Thread leaks on timeout. pebble.ProcessPool(context='spawn') + setrlimit initializer is the correct upgrade path. For current threat model (own LLM, verified doctors) ThreadPoolExecutor is acceptable.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Indirect prompt injection via medical records — JAMA 2024 study found 94.4% success rate against medical LLM systems. Patient visit descriptions, medication names, and observations are all injection vectors. Mitigate with <DADO_DB> trust boundary tags and keyword sanitization.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Temperature 0.0-0.1 optimal for single-shot code generation. Databricks BFCL: accuracy varies up to 10% between temp 0 and 0.7 for function-calling tasks. Original plan said 0.3 — update to 0.1.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: OllamaCodeClient subclasses BaseLLMClient and implements generate_with_tools() using exec. MessageService never changes. Factory selects which client at startup. This is the strategy pattern, not a flag/branch.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: AST repair (trim trailing incomplete lines, retry ast.parse) improves Llama-3.1-7B accuracy from 60.9% to 76.8% for code extraction (Schema-Aligned Parsing research). Implement _extract_code_block with repair, not just naive regex.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with research from 8 parallel agents. Key improvements: (1) exec() __builtins__:{} is NOT a sandbox — added AST validation layer blocking __subclasses__/dunder access; (2) Architecture changed to OllamaCodeClient strategy pattern — MessageService unchanged; (3) Temperature changed from 0.3 to 0.1 (Databricks BFCL research); (4) AST repair added to code extraction (76.8% vs 60.9% accuracy on 7B models); (5) Security audit surfaced 4 findings — 2 filed as emmg.6/emmg.7, 2 noted in smoke test bead; (6) Prompt injection via medical records flagged with <DADO_DB> trust boundary mitigation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Plan review found: (1) ThreadPoolExecutor timeout is false safety — cannot preempt Python thread mid-allocation; memory exhaustion via 'A'*10**9 is a real DoS vector; subprocess+RLIMIT_AS is the correct mitigation. (2) AST dunder blocklist should be blanket rule (startswith/endswith '__') not specific list. (3) get_patient_info() has same auth bypass as get_doctor_info (emmg.6) — uses user_id for logging only, not authorization. (4) Telegram webhook verify_webhook() returns True unconditionally — Critical blocker. (5) Cut factory.py, ToolResult/CodeResult dataclasses, AST repair retry, runtime prompt introspection — ~100 LOC unnecessary. (6) OllamaCodeClient should inherit from OllamaClient, not re-implement generate(). (7) System prompt injection belongs in OllamaCodeClient.generate_with_tools(), not context_service.py.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Review surfaced two additional pre-launch blockers: (1) emmg.9 — Telegram webhook no secret token validation (P1 Critical, blocks all write tools); (2) emmg.8 — get_patient_info auth bypass (same class as emmg.6). Smoke test should validate both are fixed before running write-operation tests.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: verify_webhook() now validates X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token using hmac.compare_digest for timing-safe comparison. Empty TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET bypasses validation for dev-env backward compat.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: settings imports must use the module-level settings singleton (from ...settings import settings) rather than re-instantiating Settings(), to avoid circular import and config duplication.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: get_patient_info() now joins Patient -> Visit -> Doctor and filters on Doctor.user_id == user_id before returning patient data. This is the same two-hop join used in get_recent_patients() and get_doctor_info() (emmg.6). The pattern ensures org isolation at the service layer without relying solely on RLS.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: limit is parsed in three different patterns in ToolExecutor.execute(): (1) str/int normalization block then min() cap for get_recent_patients, get_upcoming_visits, get_recent_visits; (2) direct inline arguments.get() for get_current_medications which needed a dedicated limit variable before the service call. Always apply min() after type normalization, not before.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced patient_service.get_patient_by_id(patient_id) — which had no user_id filter — with a direct SQLAlchemy query joining through Visit and Doctor. The direct query was chosen over delegating to PatientService because: (1) it co-locates the authorization logic in the service method that owns it, (2) matches the established pattern from get_recent_patients() and emmg.6, (3) avoids adding user_id params to PatientService.get_patient_by_id() which has a different responsibility.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: get_current_medications had no type-normalization block unlike the other three tools — its limit was passed directly as arguments.get('limit', 10) inline in the service call. A non-integer value from the LLM (e.g. a string) would have caused a TypeError at the service layer. The fix extracts and normalizes it first: limit = min(int(arguments.get('limit', 10)), 50). The int() conversion here could raise ValueError for non-numeric strings, but that is caught by the outer ValueError handler.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: All tools pass self.user_id for authorization. get_recent_patients() is the canonical example: joins through Doctor where Doctor.user_id == user_id to scope queries to the requesting user's context.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Tool results are added in two paths in ollama_client.py generate_with_tools(): (1) raw text tool call workaround path ~line 148 appends role=tool after a synthetic assistant message, (2) structured tool_calls path ~line 253 inside the for loop over tool_calls. Both paths share the same tool_result_str variable after PDF/string normalization.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Insurer table has org_id (nullable). RLS policy c4d5e6f7a8b9 covers insurers with allow_null=True: records with org_id IS NULL are global (visible to all orgs), records with org_id set are visible only to that org. RLS is enforced with FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY so even the service account cannot bypass it. service-layer methods (get_active_insurers, search_insurers_fuzzy) have no org_id filter but do not need one — the data model intentionally allows global insurers with NULL org_id.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used org scoping (Doctor.organization_id == requesting_doctor.organization_id) rather than self-only scoping. This allows a doctor to look up colleagues in the same org (legitimate use case) while preventing cross-org data leakage. Replaced DoctorService delegation with a direct two-step query: first resolve requesting doctor's org, then filter target doctor by org.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Wrapped tool_result_str with <DADO_DB>\n...\n</DADO_DB> at both insertion points in ollama_client.py. Added REGRAS DE SEGURANÇA block to SYSTEM_PROMPT_DOCTOR_PT in prompts.py instructing the LLM to treat DADO_DB content as data only, never as instructions. Used correct Portuguese accents (segurança, são, não). Did not tag the PDF-path message since that is a short system-generated string, not DB content — only the tool_result_str that contains patient/visit data gets tagged.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: No service-layer org_id filter added. The NULL org_id = global insurer pattern is intentional (system-wide insurers shared across all orgs). Adding org_id filter would break this. Fixed cosmetic SQLAlchemy style violation: Insurer.active -> Insurer.active.is_(True) in InsurerRepository.get_active_insurers().

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: RLS on insurers table uses allow_null policy: 'org_id IS NULL OR org_id = app.org_id'. FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is set, so the app db user cannot bypass it. This is defense-in-depth coverage — RLS enforces the correct boundary. No service-layer gap exists because the intended behavior is global insurers (NULL org_id) being visible to all orgs.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: slowapi limiter in this project is defined in middleware/rate_limiter.py and uses get_real_ip as key_func. It is registered on app.state and handles RateLimitExceeded globally. The webhook body (request.json()) is consumed inside the handler, so slowapi key_func cannot read it without body-caching middleware — making slowapi unsuitable for session-keyed Telegram rate limiting.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used an in-memory sliding-window rate limiter (module-level defaultdict of deques) instead of slowapi for the Telegram webhook. Reason: slowapi key_func receives only the Request object before the body is parsed, so extracting platform_user_id there would require reading the body twice (body caching middleware or request.body() + manual parse). Since platform_user_id is already available inside the handler after adapter.handle_update(), applying the check there avoids that complexity. No Redis is present, so in-memory is the appropriate choice. Limit: 10 messages / 60-second sliding window per Telegram user ID.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Two dispatch sites in ollama_client.py lacked tool-name allowlist checks before calling tool_executor.execute(): (1) the structured tool_calls loop (~line 236) and (2) the raw text tool call fallback path (~line 133). Both paths extracted a tool name from LLM output and dispatched it without validating against the MEDICAL_TOOLS schema. An injected or hallucinated tool name would reach ToolExecutor.execute() directly.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added allowlist check at both dispatch sites in ollama_client.py. Before calling tool_executor.execute(), code now builds allowed_tools = {t['function']['name'] for t in tools} and rejects unknown names with 'Ferramenta desconhecida.' and a warning log. Applied to: (a) structured tool_calls for-loop path, (b) raw text tool call fallback path. The tools parameter is already in scope at both sites so no signature changes were needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ToolExecutor.execute() final else clause (line 681) returns a safe Portuguese error string: "Erro: ferramenta '{tool_name}' não reconhecida" — no exception raised, no side effects. It is safe as a last-resort fallback, but the allowlist check at the call sites provides defense-in-depth and produces a warning log entry for detection.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: summarize_conversation() had no injection-resistance in its prompt. The resulting summary was stored back as role='system' (highest trust) via build_context() line 61. _format_messages_for_summary() filters to user+assistant roles only (skips tool role), but assistant messages may echo DB content from tool results. emmg.11 (DADO_DB tags) is already merged, so tool results now have tags — but the summarizer had no instruction to ignore them.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ChatMessage.user_id was nullable=False in models.py, requiring a migration (k9b0c1d2e3f4) to ALTER COLUMN to nullable=True before the user_id=None fix can work in production.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: ValueError user_friendly_message changed from str(e) verbatim to a fixed Portuguese generic message to prevent internal state leakage; raw exception is still logged server-side via logger.warning with error_id for traceability.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: sanitize_error_for_user(exc, user_friendly_message, context_dict) -> (str, str) returns (message_to_show_user, error_id); the user_friendly_message arg is shown directly to the end user, so it must never contain str(e) for unexpected exceptions.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Applied two mitigations combined. (1) Changed summary storage from role='system' to a user/assistant pair — this downgrades trust level of summarized content and prevents it from being treated as authoritative instructions. (2) Added anti-injection instruction to the summarization system prompt in Portuguese with proper accents, including explicit DADO_DB tag awareness. This is Option A+B combined: the summarizer is told to ignore DB instructions, AND the output is stored at lower trust. Option C (strip tool messages) was not needed since _format_messages_for_summary() already filters tool-role messages.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: build_context() inserts the summary before recent_messages[-10:], so it always appears early in the context window. With the fix, it is now a user/assistant exchange rather than a bare system message, which means a subsequent real system prompt still has authority over it. The summarization LLM call uses temperature=0.3 and max_tokens=150, limiting verbose injection propagation even without the prompt fix.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: OllamaClient is instantiated in a FastAPI dependency function get_llm_client() inside medsimples/backend/app/chat/routers/telegram_webhook.py line 60. That is the single routing site for emmg.4 to update.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Routing conditional placed as a TODO comment at the instantiation site rather than dead code import, since OllamaCodeClient doesn't exist yet. Import will be local (inside the function) to avoid circular import issues when emmg.4 lands.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Code-mode prompt uses literal Python def blocks with docstrings (not auto-generated) so signatures are stable across renames. Block is a module-level string constant in prompts.py.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Critical instruction is 'NUNCA use import — nenhuma importação é permitida' combined with blocking __ attributes — together they prevent sandbox escape in restricted exec environment.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: get_patient_visits and get_visit_medications are included in the prompt even though they don't yet exist in medical_service.py — they will be added when code_executor.py is created in a later bead.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: get_patient_visits and get_visit_medications and create_visit_note do not exist in MedicalService — implemented as thin wrappers: get_patient_visits calls get_recent_visits with patient_id filter, get_visit_medications calls get_visit_details and extracts medications key, create_visit_note calls update_visit_description

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: All MedicalService methods already return serialized dicts/lists, not ORM objects — no custom serializer needed in closures

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: date/datetime/timedelta exposed in namespace via module-level aliases (_date_cls etc) to avoid __import__ calls inside dict literal; BLOCKED_NAMES check applies only to user code, not module itself

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Safe builtins (list, dict, str, int, float, bool, len, range, sorted, enumerate, zip, min, max, sum, round, abs, print) added to namespace explicitly — __builtins__:{} strips everything else

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: PDF detection in code mode uses ast.literal_eval() on run_code output string — if output starts with '{' and contains 'pdf_path', parse as dict and extract path/message. Mirrors OllamaClient's isinstance(tool_result, dict) and 'pdf_path' in tool_result check.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: OllamaClient.generate() is a plain async def that calls self.client.chat() with options dict (temperature, num_predict). It does NOT accept tools= parameter — generate_with_tools() is the tool-aware method. OllamaCodeClient.generate_with_tools() calls self.generate() (no tools arg) for the single round-trip.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: SYSTEM_PROMPT_DOCTOR_CODE_MODE_PT contains a {current_date} placeholder that must be formatted before use. context_service.py is NOT called — the placeholder is formatted inline in generate_with_tools() with date.today().strftime('%d/%m/%Y'), consistent with context_service's own formatting pattern.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: All pre-conditions are now met: emmg.9 (webhook secret), emmg.8 (get_patient_info auth), emmg.6 (get_doctor_info auth), emmg.7 (limit cap) all closed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Smoke test bead requires live Ollama (localhost:11434). Code review confirms: AST validation rejects imports and dunder access, DB session closed in finally, DADO_DB tags applied, system prompt replaced inline. Full live test to be run by developer with Ollama running.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: verify_webhook() was implemented but never called in the router — the most critical gap in the security fixes. Authentication code that exists but isn't invoked is a common review-only-catchable bug.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Pydantic schema and SQLAlchemy model were updated for nullable user_id but ChatMessageCreate/Response schemas were not — schema-model sync is a common migration oversight to check.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: run_code() returns error strings (not exceptions) making success/failure indistinguishable to callers — string-based error returns are an anti-pattern when callers need to branch on failure.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Python 3 None/True/False are ast.Constant nodes, not ast.Name lookups — adding them to an exec namespace dict has no effect. They cannot be blocked by BLOCKED_NAMES either.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added verify_webhook() call at the very top of the handler body, before request.json() is awaited. This ensures unauthenticated requests are rejected before any body parsing, rate-limit tracking, DB access, or LLM inference. Early return is inside the try block but returns normally (no exception path), so the except block's update_data reference is safe. Also initialized update_data = {} before the try block so the except block's .get() doesn't NameError if verify_webhook itself throws.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Made user_id Optional[int] = None in both ChatMessageCreate and ChatMessageResponse. ChatSessionCreate.user_id and ChatSessionResponse.user_id are intentionally left as required int — sessions always belong to a user. Only messages from unlinked-session exchanges can have null user_id.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used Option A (DELETE) over Option B (NotImplementedError). Unlinked-session messages have no valid user to assign to, so deleting them is the only semantically correct action. These rows have no clinical value — they represent pre-authentication exchanges from unlinked Telegram users.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced visit-join auth with org-scoped lookup: resolve requesting doctor, then filter Patient by org_id. Patients with no visits in same org are now found correctly. Org isolation is still enforced — different-org patients still return None.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used Python 3.10+ union syntax (int | None) for type hints. Fixed _cap_limit, three patient_id params, after_date annotation, build_namespace return type, and _get_visit_medications docstring.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Log full exc at error level via logger.error before returning generic Portuguese message. The generic message prevents SQLAlchemy internals (column/table/constraint names) from leaking to Telegram.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed the finally block entirely — the outer try had no except/finally left so was also removed. DB session is owned by FastAPI get_db and closed at request teardown. SQLAlchemy tolerates double-close on pooled sessions but the architectural violation was confusing.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Guard raises ValueError (not returns error string) because this is a programming error, not a runtime data error. A caller passing an empty or non-system-prefixed messages list indicates a bug in the calling code that should fail loudly.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: session.user_id is available in _handle_command (passed as parameter). The fix is a single keyword argument addition. MessageService.handle_command already accepts user_id: Optional[int] = None.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced 'import ast as _ast' inside function body with module-level ast import. Also removed redundant isinstance(result_dict, dict) check — ast.literal_eval on a string starting with '{' that succeeds already guarantees dict.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: In Python 3, None/True/False are ast.Constant nodes, not ast.Name lookups. They are never resolved via the exec namespace dict. These three entries were dead code.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used Option A (size cap) over Option C (comment only). Added _MAX_TRACKED_SESSIONS=10_000 with oldest-key eviction. Also added a comment documenting the per-process limitation and Redis upgrade path.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Built allowed_tools as frozenset once before the iteration loop. Removed Set import since it's no longer used. frozenset is slightly faster for 'in' checks than set.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed string->date parsing entirely. date/datetime/timedelta are already in the namespace so LLM code should pass date objects directly. Silent failure on malformed strings was worse than a TypeError.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added comment inline after run_code() call explaining why DADO_DB is absent in code-mode (single round-trip — DB data never re-enters the model context window).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Chose Option A — removed print from namespace and removed redirect_stdout/io.StringIO machinery entirely. Single result channel: assign to 'result' variable. Also removed 'io' and 'redirect_stdout' imports. -10 LOC.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Migration FK constraint name is 'chat_messages_user_id_fkey' — this is the SQLAlchemy/Alembic default naming convention for this column and table. Checked original migration to confirm no custom name was set.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: OllamaCodeClient generate_with_tools() bypasses context_service — system prompt is replaced inline; conversation context is NOT injected. This is by design.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: SYSTEM_PROMPT_DOCTOR_CODE_MODE_PT uses .format(current_date=...) — any f-string literal dict access like {v['date']} must be escaped as {{v['date']}} or KeyError is raised at runtime.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: LLM_MODE env var selects client: 'code' -> OllamaCodeClient, anything else -> OllamaClient. Confirmed via llm_processing_started INFO log showing client class name.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: 686-second hang + empty response was OllamaCodeClient returning empty content after Ollama was slow. Empty guard added: result['response'] or fallback message.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: PESQUISAR_PACIENTE_MENU and PESQUISAR_CONSULTA_MENU are distinct MenuState values from SEARCH_PATIENT_BY_NAME and SEARCH_VISIT_BY_PATIENT — both need to be handled in handle_text_input() for menu typing to work.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Telegram retries webhook updates after 60s timeout. With slow LLM (8+ min), multiple retries arrive. The retry of a slow LLM message landed while menu was in SEARCH_PATIENT_BY_NAME state, so old query text was used as patient name. Fix: deduplicate by update_id with 5-min TTL in an in-process dict.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: _is_duplicate_update() uses time.monotonic() and evicts expired entries at capacity (1000 entries). Same eviction pattern as the existing rate limiter.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: MedicationParser.parse_single_medication() and parse_medication_list() call llm_client.generate() — not generate_with_tools(). With a slow model this blocks the menu flow for minutes, causing Telegram to retry and creating the replay problem.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Reverted regex parser back to LLM after switching to llama3.1:8b. Regex was too rigid for varied doctor input formats. LLM is more flexible and fast enough on llama3.1:8b (~400ms).

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: InvoicePayments showed error to user even though payment was saved — likely a transient network/CSRF issue. Without a submitting guard the user could re-submit. Fixed with submitting state flag that disables the Save button during the request.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: axiosConfig.ts interceptor handles CSRF for all mutations globally via Object.assign on config.headers. Components do NOT need to set X-CSRF-Token manually — the interceptor covers them. Exception: if addCsrfToHeaders throws, the catch silently continues without the token.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: MUI theme warning/info colors must use darker shades (amber-600, sky-600) for WCAG compliance on white backgrounds — the default yellow/light-blue fail contrast checks

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: KPICard values use color.dark instead of color.main so numeric values have sufficient contrast on white card backgrounds

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: background.default #EEF2F7 creates visible card separation vs #F8FAFC which made cards invisible against the page background

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Welcome banners need a solid background color (#F0FDFA) + explicit border — rgba(color, 0.04) is effectively invisible in light mode

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Follow existing migration naming convention. Check alembic/versions/ for latest revision to set correct down_revision.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: db.commit() destroys RLS context — use db.add() then db.flush() then db.commit() pattern (never commit mid-transaction). See medsimples-su8p learnings.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: MagicToken atomic single-use: UPDATE...RETURNING with advisory lock. See user_service.py consume_magic_token() for reference implementation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: active=True must be explicitly set for invite-based registration — standard registration sets active=False. The invite token click validates the email.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: CogniTest backend forwards user JWT as Bearer token when proxying to MedSimples. See medsimples_client.py for existing proxy patterns.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Two-phase token pattern — GET /validate on page load (safe for email scanners), POST /accept on form submit. This prevents Outlook SafeLinks from consuming the single-use token.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with 7 parallel research agents. Key improvements: (1) Invite model improved with partial unique index, consumed_at column, proper FK ondelete, InviteStatus enum; (2) accept_invite() must use set_superadmin_context() before writes since endpoint is unauthenticated; (3) Two distinct auth modes for proxy endpoints — JWT-forward for admin invite creation, X-Webhook-Secret for public accept/validate; (4) Email dispatch must use BackgroundTasks to be non-blocking.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Plan review found 9 important issues. Key fixes applied to child beads: (1) partial index must use text("status = 'pending'") not Python enum comparison — SQLAlchemy silently produces invalid WHERE clause; (2) Drop MagicToken FK from Invite — store token_hash directly, eliminates join and dual-expiry ambiguity; (3) Drop InviteStatus.expired — derivable from expires_at < now(), storing it requires deferred background job causing stale state; (4) set_org_rls_context() mandatory in accept_invite(), set_superadmin_context() forbidden (bypasses all RLS); (5) existing-org-member accept must hard-fail (409), not silently migrate user; (6) GET /validate must return masked email only, no org_role; (7) hmac.compare_digest() required in verify_webhook_secret() — existing code uses \!= (timing-vulnerable); (8) Drop advisory lock — atomic UPDATE...RETURNING CTE is sufficient; (9) cognitest_frontend_url must be added to Settings with validation in same PR as email template.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Kept SPA mode (ssr=false) — SvelteKit server-side cookie APIs are not available without SSR. Instead, FastAPI sets the httpOnly ct_token cookie on register response and the frontend uses credentials: 'include' on all API fetches. The backend get_current_doctor dependency now accepts the token from either the ct_token cookie (priority) or the Authorization Bearer header for backward compatibility.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: When FastAPI is the static file server AND the API server (same origin in production), Set-Cookie from the FastAPI /api/v1/auth/register endpoint is accepted by the browser without CORS credentials concerns. In dev the cookie SameSite=Strict + Secure=False works because frontend (5174) and backend (8001) are different ports on same host — browser still sends the cookie to same-host requests.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: When using alembic op.create_table() with postgresql.ENUM(create_type=False), you must still create the type explicitly before the table via a connection.execute(DO $$...$$) block (idempotent). Standard sa.Enum(..., create_type=False) does NOT reliably suppress the auto-CREATE TYPE in older SQLAlchemy versions — use postgresql.ENUM instead. Also: orgrole is NOT a native PG enum type in this codebase; users.org_role is VARCHAR(20), so Invite.org_role should also be String(20) in the migration (keep Enum(OrgRole) in the model for Python type safety only).

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: cognitest_frontend_url validator must use @field_validator (Pydantic v2) not @validator; validates no trailing slash at startup so misconfigured URL fails fast rather than producing silent broken links in invite emails

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: org_invite.html follows magic_link.html exactly — extends _base.html, HTML table CTA button with inline styles, fallback plain link below hr, all Portuguese with proper accents. send_invite_email() mirrors send_magic_link() structure: guard on email_enabled + resend_api_key, render template, asyncio.to_thread(_send_sync), log+swallow exception

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: set_org_rls_context must be called AFTER the atomic UPDATE...RETURNING (invite consumption), not before, because the RETURNING result still needs to be read from the same transaction. The RLS context is needed only for the subsequent User/Doctor writes.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added set_org_rls_context(db, org_id, org_role) to auth/dependencies.py as a proper helper alongside set_rls_context/set_superadmin_context, rather than inlining raw SQL in the service. Keeps the RLS context API consistent and centralized.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: email_service.py already had send_invite_email() with signature (email, invite_token, org_name, inviter_name, org_role) — implemented prior to this bead. The service must pass org_name so the background task can fetch it from DB before commit (since the invite record is already committed at that point).

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: The ct_token httpOnly cookie holds the MedSimples JWT in CogniTest. For JWT-forward proxy endpoints, extract it via Cookie(alias=CT_COOKIE_NAME) and pass as Authorization: Bearer. The oauth2_scheme bearer fallback is also checked (same priority as get_current_doctor).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Public proxy endpoints (validate/accept) use _get_headers() which injects X-Webhook-Secret for service-to-service auth with MedSimples — no user JWT is needed or forwarded. org_id for invite creation is derived from the authenticated doctor's session, not from the request body, preventing cross-org invite injection.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: verify_webhook_secret() now uses hmac.compare_digest() (constant-time) instead of \!= to prevent timing attacks. import hmac moved to module level.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: CogniTest auth is httpOnly cookie (ct_token), not localStorage. The bead description mentioned cognitest_token in localStorage but this was outdated. All API calls use credentials: 'include' and auth guard calls /api/v1/auth/me.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Placed invite form at /settings/team as a standalone SvelteKit route (not inside the (app) group) because (app) has no layout and the new route needed the same auth-guard-in-onMount pattern used by commissions. Nav link added to root +layout.svelte via a best-effort /auth/me check; hidden when unauthenticated or on error.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used ?token= param detection in the existing /register route rather than a separate route. The {#if inviteToken} block in the template renders a completely different UI (no 3-step wizard, no org fields) while reusing shared password strength logic.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: The CogniTest /invites/accept proxy returns raw JSON {access_token, token_type} (not a cookie). The auth/register endpoint wraps the token in an httpOnly cookie. Invite flow must store token in localStorage manually. InviteAcceptRequest schema only has {token, email, password, name} — no CRM/phone at the accept layer.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Review found 3 P1 blockers: (1) accept proxy doesn't set httpOnly cookie — no session after invite acceptance; (2) token burned before email validation — wrong email permanently voids invite; (3) FORCE RLS on invites table blocks unauthenticated token lookup — accept always returns 404. Plus 2 P2 and 2 P3 issues.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: moved email normalization before UPDATE and added to WHERE clause — atomic validation prevents token burning on wrong email

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: split FOR ALL policy into SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE — SELECT allows unauthenticated reads (token_hash is cryptographic security), INSERT requires org context, UPDATE allows all (WHERE clause provides selectivity)

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: NULLIF(current_setting('app.org_id', true), '') IS NULL check needed because current_setting returns empty string (not NULL) when unset — direct ::integer cast would throw invalid input syntax

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Created auth/cookie.py with set_auth_cookie() and COOKIE_SECURE. Kept _set_auth_cookie in auth.py originally; moved to cookie.py to share with invites.py. Both auth.py and invites.py now import from auth/cookie.py — no circular imports since cookie.py only imports from dependencies.py and settings.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: accept_invite() now mirrors register() exactly: add Response param, call set_auth_cookie(response, token) on success, strip access_token from body dict before returning JSONResponse.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: kept 7 days (not 24h per spec) — invitees need time to register; added inline comment documenting the deviation

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: used native_enum=False on Enum(OrgRole) to match VARCHAR(20) in migration — keeps Python type safety without requiring a PG enum type

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: added effective_invite_hmac_key property that falls back to secret_key — backward compatible, allows independent rotation

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Pydantic @property works differently than @computed_field — used plain @property since it doesn't need to be serialized

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: MedSimples POST /token uses OAuth2PasswordRequestForm — httpx must use data= not json= for form encoding. Returns {access_token, token_type}.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Cookie + strip token pattern: add Response param to endpoint, call set_auth_cookie(response, token), return {k:v for k,v in body.items() if k != 'access_token'}. See register() and accept_invite() in auth.py.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: goto('/') after register currently lands on the marketing page (routes/+page.svelte), not the app. The correct post-auth destination is /commissions.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: No auth guard exists yet on /(app) routes. /login page is a new route at routes/login/+page.svelte. Auth guard on /commissions can be a separate bead if needed later.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Plan review found: (1) login() must use request_magic_link pattern (shared client), NOT register() which has a per-call client leak; (2) login() must use data= not json= and must NOT call _get_headers() which forces Content-Type: application/json; (3) LoginRequest needs max_length on password to prevent 10MB payload forwarding; (4) JWT error leak in dependencies.py line 47 should be fixed

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Applied plan review feedback. 7 recommendations applied across 2 beads + 1 new bead (ib8d.3). Key changes: (1) login() must use request_magic_link shared-client pattern; (2) LoginRequest.password needs max_length; (3) JWT error leak fix added to ib8d.1 scope; (4) Auth guard bead created as ib8d.3. Skipped: CSRF (samesite=strict adequate), bcrypt cap (MedSimples owns hashing), httpx pool sizing (pre-existing, separate concern).

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: MedSimples POST /token uses OAuth2PasswordRequestForm — httpx must use data= (form encoding) not json=. The _get_headers() helper must NOT be called for login because it forces Content-Type: application/json, which breaks form-encoded requests. login() uses self._shared_client following the request_magic_link/create_invite pattern to avoid per-call AsyncClient resource leaks.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: login endpoint returns 422 (not 401) for bad credentials to match the spec and avoid leaking whether the email exists. Upstream 5xx and connection errors both surface as 502 with a generic Portuguese message. JWT error detail leak in dependencies.py fixed to return 'Authentication required' instead of the raw JWTError string.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: goto('/') after register/invite-accept was landing on the marketing homepage (routes/+page.svelte). Fixed both handleSubmit and handleInviteSubmit in register/+page.svelte to goto('/commissions'). New /login route at routes/login/+page.svelte uses credentials: 'include' for cookie auth, 3 error cases (429, 502/503, other 4xx), clears password on error.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: SvelteKit (app) group layout.ts auth guard — ssr=false + browser guard + fetch /auth/me with credentials:include; redirect(302,'/login') on 401. SvelteKit redirect() throws a Response-like object, so the catch must re-throw when e has a .status property, otherwise the redirect is silently swallowed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Review found 2 P1 bugs: (1) login returns 200 silently when MedSimples 200 has no access_token — treat missing token as 502; (2) rate limiter uses get_remote_address instead of get_real_ip — effectively non-functional behind a reverse proxy in production.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: SvelteKit redirect() throws a Redirect object, not a Response — use isRedirect() from @sveltejs/kit, never instanceof Response, to re-throw in catch blocks.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: treat missing access_token on 200 response as 502 — silent cookie-less 200 causes a confusing redirect loop in the frontend

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: imported get_real_ip from main.py — it walks X-Forwarded-For for trusted proxies and is the only correct IP resolution in this deployment

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: replaced instanceof Response with isRedirect() from @sveltejs/kit — SvelteKit redirect() throws a Redirect object not a Response

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: logout button added to root +layout.svelte alongside the existing team-settings link; isAuthenticated flag set after /auth/me succeeds so logout only appears for authenticated users; uses goto('/login') after POST to /auth/logout

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: removed handleKeyDown — form on:submit handles Enter natively; added if(loading) return guard as first line of handleSubmit; removed svelte-ignore comment that was masking the misplaced handler

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: set Cache-Control once at function start rather than per-exception — cleaner and guaranteed to apply on all paths

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: password cleared in each error branch not in finally — only clear on failure, preserve on network error so user can retry without retyping

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE in src/lib/constants.ts — single source of truth for post-auth redirect

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: SameSite=Strict suppresses the cookie on the first request after any POST->navigation sequence (login -> goto('/commissions')). The browser considers the context changed and withholds the cookie. SameSite=Lax fixes this while still blocking cross-site POST CSRF.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: delete_cookie() must use identical SameSite/Secure/path attributes as the original set_cookie() — attribute mismatch causes the browser to silently ignore the deletion.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: throw redirect() inside a try/catch is swallowed in SvelteKit SPA mode during client-side navigation even when isRedirect(e) re-throws. Fix: store fetch result in a variable, close the try/catch, then throw redirect() unconditionally outside any catch block.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Circular import: routers/auth.py cannot import from main.py because main.py imports routers/auth.py via routers/__init__.py. Moved get_real_ip to core/ip.py to break the cycle.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: goto() in SvelteKit triggers the /(app)/+layout.ts load function before the browser commits the Set-Cookie header from the login response. window.location.href forces a full page reload, ensuring the cookie is in the jar before /auth/me is called.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: SECRET_KEY must be identical between MedSimples and CogniTest in production. CogniTest decodes MedSimples-issued JWTs directly — if the signing keys differ, every /auth/me call returns 401 with no useful log message. Root cause of the production login failure.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added jwt_decode_failed log with JWTError type and detail string in _decode_token(). This makes key mismatches immediately visible as 'Signature verification failed' rather than a silent 401.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Auth guard redirect should always include a reason param (/login?reason=session_expired). Login page reads the param on init and pre-populates the error state — no store, no flash mechanism needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Exact SQL error: commissions.doctor_id = '8'::UUID — PostgreSQL rejects integer string cast to UUID. Query fails at runtime for all commission lookups after password login.

author: Roberto Mello
text:
INVESTIGATION: Sessions list (routers/sessions.py:225) does not join Patient — SessionResponse has no cached_name. Need to add selectinload(TestSession.patient) and include PatientInfo in response.

FACT: CogniTest Patient model has medsimples_patient_id (UUID) but MedSimples sends integer IDs — this is the same UUID mismatch as medsimples-jsx8. Patient search proxy must handle this.

FACT: MedSimples patient search is GET /patients?search= protected by RLS via get_current_user_with_rls. CogniTest cannot call this with webhook secret — must forward the doctor's Bearer token.

PATTERN: Revenue card must gracefully handle missing commissions table. Commissions router has no try/except for table-not-found. Frontend should catch the error and show empty state.

FACT: CreditsWidget.svelte already exists at frontend/src/lib/components/CreditsWidget.svelte — reuse on dashboard.

FACT: Auth layout returns {} — dashboard page must fetch /auth/me itself to get doctor identity (user_id, org_id) for scoping queries.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with 7 parallel agents: architecture-strategist, security-sentinel, performance-oracle, frontend-design+design-taste (combined), FastAPI proxy research, SQLAlchemy selectinload research, SvelteKit modal/debounce research, Alembic migration research. Key improvements: (1) Security — 3 critical findings in existing sessions.py unrelated to new feature but must be fixed before launch; (2) Performance — use Promise.all for dashboard calls, selectinload pattern confirmed, add composite DB index; (3) Design — full design spec from design-taste-frontend skill with WCAG-compliant color palette.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: create_session (sessions.py:120) has no authentication dependency — any caller who knows a valid patient UUID can create sessions for any patient. IDOR vulnerability. Must add get_current_doctor + patient org check BEFORE shipping bead 0ekz.4.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: get_or_create_default_org (sessions.py:33) uses db.query(Organization).first() — returns whichever org is first in DB, not the authenticated doctor's org. All sessions created under wrong org in multi-tenant mode. Critical data isolation failure. Fix in bead 0ekz.4.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: update_session (sessions.py:279) has no access key validation — any caller who knows a session UUID can change status to 'completed', bypassing payment. File a separate bead for this.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Shared httpx.AsyncClient via lifespan (app.state.http_client) — NOT per-request. Per-request clients incur TLS handshake cost (~50-150ms) per MedSimples call. MedSimplesClient.get_patient/get_doctor/validate_token still use per-call pattern and should be migrated.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Rate limiting for patient search should use doctor.user_id as key, not IP. Authenticated endpoints behind NAT share one IP — per-IP limits break legitimate users. Raise to 60/minute to accommodate 300ms-debounced typeahead search.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: GET /tests/definitions should use an in-process module-level cache (TTL=5min) since test definitions are admin-seeded and effectively never change between deploys. Avoids a DB round-trip on every dashboard load. Implementation: module-level _cache + _cache_ts in a test_definition_cache.py service, check time.monotonic() against TTL before querying. NOT Redis — the full active set is <20 rows. Also: GET /commissions/current-month currently fires a second DB query if tests_completed > 0 to fetch Commission.status — combine into one query using conditional aggregation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Applied plan review feedback. 20 recommendations applied across 5 beads. Key changes: (1) Simplified 0ekz.1 — dropped X-Upstream-Available header, PatientSearchResult Pydantic schema, schemas/patient.py file, and per-user rate limiting; (2) Added auth + org scoping requirement to list_sessions in 0ekz.2 — critical for multi-tenancy and prevents full table scan; (3) Added 3 new composite indexes to 0ekz.5 — clinician_user_id+created_at for sessions dashboard, doctor_id+period for commissions, status+created_at consolidation from 0ekz.2; (4) Replaced XOR Pydantic validator in 0ekz.4 with inline resolution + added type-safety for org_id (int cast) + added get_ct_org_for_doctor utility; (5) Updated 0ekz.3 to extract OrderTestModal.svelte and use lib/utils/patients.ts path. Pre-existing critical security gaps flagged for separate beads: list_sessions/update_session unauth, credits endpoints unauth, results endpoints unauth.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: list_sessions had no auth at all — any unauthenticated client could enumerate sessions across all orgs. Fixed by adding Depends(get_current_doctor) and filtering by clinician_user_id.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: clinician_user_id is UUID() column (as_uuid unset). DoctorInfo.user_id is str, so UUID(doctor.user_id) cast is required in the filter to avoid type mismatch.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: selectinload placed before .filter() to match options-before-filter requirement; count() called before offset/limit so pagination total is accurate.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: MedSimplesClient.search_patients uses self._shared_client (shared httpx.AsyncClient from app.state) to avoid per-call TLS handshake cost. Falls back to per-request client with follow_redirects=False only if shared client is unavailable.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ct_token cookie is a valid MedSimples JWT — extracting it via request.cookies.get(CT_COOKIE_NAME) and forwarding as Authorization: Bearer gives the upstream MedSimples RLS context without sharing the webhook secret.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Authorization header value is never logged (token leakage risk). Upstream errors log status_code only. Empty list returned for all non-200 responses and network errors — no X-Upstream-Available header.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: organizations.medsimples_org_id was already converted to Integer in migration 3e6948c70b83 (revises 019). The Python model also already had Integer. Only patients.medsimples_patient_id remained as UUID in both model and DB.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: When a nullable UUID column has all-NULL rows, USING NULL::INTEGER is the safe USING clause for UUID->Integer ALTER TYPE. No complex casting needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: SQLAlchemy UniqueConstraint in __table_args__ cannot express partial (WHERE IS NOT NULL) indexes — use raw CREATE UNIQUE INDEX in the migration and keep a comment in the model pointing to the migration.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: create_session had no auth dep — IDOR fixed by adding Depends(get_current_doctor). get_or_create_default_org used db.query(Organization).first() which is wrong in multi-tenant; replaced with get_ct_org_for_doctor() that filters by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: MedSimples API call (get_patient) happens before the DB flush for Patient creation, avoiding holding a transaction open during a network call. Patient data is cached best-effort — None values are tolerated.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: PatientService.get_or_create_by_medsimples_id uses IntegrityError retry (max 2 attempts) to handle TOCTOU race on concurrent session creation for the same medsimples_patient_id. After IntegrityError, rollback is called and a fresh SELECT retrieves the winning record.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: org isolation enforced after patient resolution: patient.organization_id != org.id → 403. Both resolution paths (patient_id UUID and medsimples_patient_id int) converge to resolved_patient_id before any session or battery creation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE lives in cognitest/frontend/src/lib/constants.ts — single source of truth used by both login and register pages via window.location.href / goto()

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: The (app) layout.ts already calls /auth/me on every load and redirects to /login on 401 — dashboard +page.svelte does NOT need its own auth fetch, only data fetches in onMount. Used Promise.allSettled so one failing API (e.g. missing commissions table) does not block the whole dashboard.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: The (app) layout.ts already calls /auth/me and redirects on 401 — dashboard page uses Promise.allSettled so a failing commissions API does not block the whole page

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: OrderTestModal uses 3-step linear flow (patient->test->confirm->success). Success state inline in modal per spec, no toast

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: OrderTestModal sends test_id (singular string) but SessionCreate expects test_ids (list). Pydantic silently ignores unknown fields — session created with no test assignment. Always add extra='forbid' to schemas that handle external input.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Patient.UniqueConstraint in __table_args__ and the partial unique index in migration are contradictory — alembic autogenerate will detect a diff and re-add the full (non-partial) constraint, undoing the intentional partial index.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: PatientService.db.rollback() inside get_or_create_by_medsimples_id() rolls back the entire outer create_session transaction, including any credit deductions that already ran. Use db.begin_nested() (savepoint) instead.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Router-level Limiter(key_func=get_remote_address) is bypassed by X-Forwarded-For spoofing when behind a proxy. Always use get_real_ip (the project's proxy-aware implementation) for all rate limiters.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Migration revision ID 'a1b2c3d4e5f6' is a hand-typed placeholder, not an alembic-generated hash. Must be replaced before production deploy. Also verify commissions columns (doctor_id, period_year, period_month) exist before running ix_commissions_doctor_period index creation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Pydantic silently ignores unknown fields by default — test_id was sent but test_ids expected, so sessions were created with no test assignment. Fixed by (1) changing frontend payload from test_id to test_ids array and (2) adding model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid') to SessionCreate so future mismatches surface as 422 errors instead of silent data loss.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: SQLAlchemy UniqueConstraint cannot express partial (WHERE IS NOT NULL) indexes. Replace with Index(..., unique=True, postgresql_where=text('col IS NOT NULL')) in __table_args__ so alembic autogenerate sees an exact match to the migration's CREATE UNIQUE INDEX and emits no spurious diff.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: commissions index (ix_commissions_doctor_period) wrapped in DO block checking column existence. Original 017a migration created commissions without doctor_id/period_year/period_month; those columns belong to the redesigned Commission model but no migration adds them — so the index would fail on databases that ran the old schema. Conditional guard is safest without requiring a separate schema migration. Also replaced placeholder revision ID 'a1b2c3d4e5f6' with 'c4b8e1f2d9a7' and renamed the file accordingly.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Serial guard for debounced async search — increment a module-level counter before each fetch, capture the value in closure, discard result if counter has advanced. copyLink timeout stored in copyLinkTimeout variable and cleared in onDestroy alongside searchTimeout.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: submitError state variable added to OrderTestModal — reset to null at handleConfirm entry and on goBack(), set to Portuguese error string in catch block, displayed as red text in confirm step template. Keeps error scoped to the confirm step and auto-clears on retry or navigation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Migration downgrade() for lossy type conversions (UUID->int, etc.) must raise NotImplementedError with a recovery instruction rather than pass silently. A silent pass leaves Alembic thinking the rollback succeeded while the schema still reflects the upgraded state, causing subsequent upgrades to fail on non-idempotent DDL. Also: all CREATE INDEX statements in upgrade() should use IF NOT EXISTS so the migration is re-entrant (safe to retry after partial failures).

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Replaced self.db.rollback() + retry loop with db.begin_nested() savepoint. On IntegrityError the context manager rolls back only to the savepoint, leaving the outer transaction (and any prior credit deductions) intact. Single attempt suffices; SELECT after the except block retrieves the winning concurrent record.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Router-level Limiter instances in patients.py, sessions.py, and results.py were using get_remote_address (slowapi default). Replaced with get_real_ip from core/ip.py in all three. Also removed the duplicate get_client_ip() helper in sessions.py — replaced its single call site (consent IP capture) with get_real_ip(request) to eliminate duplication.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Auth strategy per endpoint type: (1) results.py — all four endpoints (session results, result by id, summary, pdf) use Depends(get_current_doctor) + get_ct_org_for_doctor() to verify session.organization_id matches doctor's CT org. For get_result by result_id, the parent session is fetched to perform the org check. (2) credits.py — all endpoints (GET summary, full, check + POST add) use Depends(get_current_doctor) + _enforce_org_access() which compares int(doctor.org_id) == organization_id. The add endpoint is not truly superadmin-only since DoctorInfo carries no superadmin claim — org match is enforced so only that org's own doctors can grant themselves credits (a limitation to revisit when superadmin concept is added to JWT). (3) sessions.py list_sessions — already had auth from a prior bead; verified and left intact.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: create_session already had get_current_doctor dep (line 103) and org isolation check (patient.organization_id != org.id → 403) at lines 176-178. Fix from bead 0ekz.4 was already in place. No changes needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Access key validation added to update_session by making access_key a required field on SessionUpdate schema (not optional), then calling validate_access_key(session, update_data.access_key) immediately after the 404 check and before any status or device_info mutation. This reuses the existing validate_access_key() helper which also allows DEV_ACCESS_KEY bypass.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed clinician_user_id from SessionCreate schema (extra='forbid' would have rejected it anyway after removal). In create_session, replaced session_data.clinician_user_id with UUID(doctor.user_id) from the authenticated JWT. Also removed the session_data.clinician_user_id guard on the notification send — the notification should fire whenever requires_payment is true and no credit was used, regardless of whether a field was in the request body.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: org_utils.py status code corrections — malformed org_id in JWT raises 401 (authentication issue) not 403; org not enrolled raises 403 with generic message; ms_org_id is logged server-side via logger.warning() and never included in HTTPException detail to prevent internal ID leakage

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Extracted shared API_BASE export from $lib/utils/api.ts (re-exports internal API_BASE_URL). Extracted testDisplayName + TestDef type into $lib/utils/tests.ts — shared by dashboard page and OrderTestModal. Hoisted statusLabel object literal to module-level const STATUS_LABEL to avoid per-call object creation. Replaced 7 hardcoded Portuguese strings in OrderTestModal and dashboard with i18n keys (dashboard.modal.noPatients, noTests, patient, test, linkCopied, submitError, dashboard.orderTest.description) — added all keys to both pt-BR.json and en.json.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Minor Python hygiene: (1) moved in-function 'from datetime import date' to top-level imports in sessions.py; (2) changed getattr(request.app.state, 'http_client', None) to direct attribute access to match patients.py pattern and surface startup misconfiguration; (3) corrected PatientInfo.cached_dob type from datetime|None to date|None to match DB column type; (4) renamed unused current_doctor param to _ in patients.py search endpoint.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added user_id_uuid: UUID field computed at __init__ time in DoctorInfo (cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py). This parses the UUID string once on token decode rather than once per DB query. sessions.py now references doctor.user_id_uuid in both list_sessions (filter) and create_session (model construction), eliminating UUID(doctor.user_id) per request.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: OrderTestModal dispatches close event with { sessionCreated: boolean }. Success step footer button sends true; all other close paths (X, Escape, backdrop, cancel) send false. Dashboard on:close handler calls loadDashboard() only when sessionCreated is true, avoiding redundant fetches on cancel.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Review found 2 critical frontend bugs — success step unreachable due to misplaced {#if submitError} block (0ekz.22), and spinner permanently stuck due to searching not reset on serial guard return (0ekz.23). Both would have been caught by any manual test of the Order Test flow.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: access_key should only appear in create_session response, never in list/detail responses. Exposing the auth token in collection endpoints multiplies the attack surface by the number of sessions a doctor has.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: State-machine transition guards are missing on all session status endpoints. AWAITING_PAYMENT sessions can be jumped directly to COMPLETED via the patient-facing update_session endpoint using a valid access_key.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: {#if submitError} was placed after the confirm step's closing div, making success step a child of the error check — it only rendered when submitError was non-null, which never happens after success.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Used Pydantic v2 Field(exclude=True) on access_key in SessionResponseDoctor. Inheritance with exclude is cleaner than redefining all fields. Only create_session retains SessionResponse with access_key included. list_sessions now uses SessionResponseDoctor via SessionListResponse.items type update.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Added selectinload(TestResult.test_definition) to all TestResult queries in results.py (get_session_results, get_session_summary) and pdf_generator.py (_get_results_data). Each route now issues 2 queries total (1 for results + 1 batch selectinload for test_definitions) instead of N+1. Also fixed a latent bug in pdf_generator._get_results_data: .filter(TestResult.status.value == 'completed') was comparing a Python string to a Python string (always True), corrected to .filter(TestResult.status == 'completed') which generates the proper SQL WHERE clause.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: USING NULL::INTEGER evaluates to NULL for EVERY row, silently nulling all data. The correct expression USING medsimples_patient_id::text::integer preserves integer-valued strings and aborts cleanly if any non-numeric value exists — strictly safer.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Serial guard must reset searching=false before returning. Otherwise the first in-flight request that loses the race leaves searching=true permanently — spinner stuck, empty state blocked.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS dict maps current_status -> set of allowed next statuses. AWAITING_PAYMENT intentionally absent — no direct client transition from it. complete_session and abandon_session get inline guards since they're dedicated endpoints (not generic status update).

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: All close paths (backdrop, Escape, X button) must check step === 'success' before dispatching sessionCreated. This ensures the dashboard refresh fires regardless of HOW the modal is dismissed after a successful session creation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: list_sessions now filters on both clinician_user_id AND organization_id. Used get_ct_org_for_doctor() to resolve the org from the authenticated doctor, matching the pattern used in other session endpoints.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: NotificationLog.cognitest_payment_id and test_session_id were String(36) but FKed to UUID PKs — PostgreSQL rejects this with DatatypeMismatch. Fix: Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), ForeignKey(...)). The String import stays because other columns still use it.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced 29 broken/conflicting migration files (001-019 + hash-named) with a single autogenerated migration using alembic revision --autogenerate against an empty DB. This is the correct approach when a migration chain has become unfixable — nuke and regenerate from models.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: PostgreSQL 18 has native uuidv7() function — no extension needed. Autogenerate picks this up correctly as server_default=text('uuidv7()').

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Migration chain had 4 compounding failures: (1) DuplicateTable on commission_rates (016 already created it), (2) DatatypeMismatch on org_credits FK (UUID vs VARCHAR timing), (3) CannotCoerce integer→uuid on commission tables (017a used int PKs not UUID), (4) NotificationLog String(36) FK type mismatch. All caused by incremental migrations that diverged from models.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Seed migrations were deleted along with schema migrations. Reconstruct from git history: git show <old-commit>:path/to/migration.py. All 16 test definitions recovered this way.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: display_name is NOT NULL on test_definitions — must be included in bulk_insert rows. Old seed migrations pre-dated this column so they didn't include it. Build display_name lookup dict and inject with list comprehension: [{**d, 'display_name': NAMES[d['code']]} for d in defs].

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: TestRunner had camelCase branches (digitSpan, reactionTime, trailMaking) but DB codes are snake_case (digit_span_forward, reaction_time_simple, trail_making_a). These codes never matched — tests would fall through to the placeholder. All other tests already had dual-spelling branches (e.g. 'snap_iv' || 'snapIV'). Fixed by adding snake_case variants to the three mismatched branches.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: API returns display_name field in TestDefinitionResponse but TestDef type in utils/tests.ts only declared id/name/name_key. testDisplayName() fell through to name_key (i18n key string) instead of Portuguese name. Fix: add display_name to type and check it first in the ?? chain.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: patient_id filter joins Invoice->InvoiceVisit->Visit via the same join pattern as insurer_id. Both use query.join(Invoice.invoice_visits).join(InvoiceVisit.visit) then filter on Visit column. No separate join needed since InvoiceVisit and Visit are already in the join chain.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Used cancelled flag in useEffect cleanup instead of AbortController — cachedAxiosGet doesn't support AbortController, so we prevent stale state updates with a boolean flag instead.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Lazy tab activation uses invoicesTabActivated boolean: set to true on first click (tab index 3), then PatientInvoicesTab renders and mounts, triggering its own fetch. cachedAxiosGet handles re-fetch prevention on subsequent tab visits.

author: Roberto Mello
text:
LEARNED: DB enum types were created UPPERCASE in migration but Python SessionStatus uses lowercase values. Dev DB had no sessions so the mismatch was never caught. Always verify enum values match between migration and model immediately after creating enums.

FACT: cognitest dev DB is at 10.10.0.47:5432:cognitestdev as user cognitestdev.

INVESTIGATION: MedSimplesClient has two classes of methods: (1) newer ones that correctly check self._shared_client, (2) 8 older ones that create new httpx.AsyncClient per call ignoring the shared pool. NotificationService also creates MedSimplesClient() with no http_client arg.

FACT: notification_service.py:802 does int(str(session.clinician_user_id)) — this raises ValueError for any UUID5-derived clinician ID. WhatsApp notifications silently fail for all sessions because the error is swallowed by create_session try/except.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Archive pdf_templates feature on a git branch rather than deleting. The 3 tables (pdf_templates, pdf_template_fields, pdf_template_versions) stay in DB for now; code removed from main branch. Clean break without destructive migration.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Logo storage via logo_path column added to organizations and doctors tables. Migration adds nullable logo_path (Text) to both. No new tables.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Logo resolution chain = doctor.logo_path > organization.logo_path > static/logo.png (system fallback). All code-generated PDFs follow this chain.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: rx_templates.file_path is nullable and currently repurposed as logo_path concept. In new design file_path is NOT used for logos — logos go on org/doctor. rx_templates/rx_template_types remain the template catalog as-is.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: All PDF types in scope: prescriptions, visit summaries, invoices/receipts. Logo injected into the ReportLab header layout (replaces the hardcoded static/logo.png path used today).

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: PDF generator resolves logo via a helper get_logo_path(doctor, org) -> str. Falls back through chain. Existing code in medical_service.py, invoice PDF, etc. all call this helper instead of hardcoding logo path.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Current code checks os.path.exists(logo_path) before using logo. New helper must preserve this pattern — return the first existing path in the chain.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Logo used in 5 PDF functions — medical_service.py:1298 (visit summary), invoice_service.py:834 (recibo), invoice_service.py:1038 (issued invoices report), invoice_service.py:1038+ (unpaid invoices report). Prescription PDF (medical_service.py:1501) currently has NO logo — add it as part of this work.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Latest migration is n2c3d4e5f6a7_fix_invites_rls_policy.py — use as down_revision for the logo_path migration.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Existing file upload pattern lives in routers/organizations.py upload_certificate() — UploadFile + File(...) + service method. Replicate for logo endpoints.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: The logo file must be serveable from the frontend. Need to verify if storage_path files are already served as static files, or if a GET endpoint is needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Logo serving uses the existing storage_path static mount. No S3 or separate GET endpoint needed for v1. If storage moves to S3 later, only logo_service.py get_logo_path() needs updating. Keeping it simple.

author: Roberto Mello
text: INVESTIGATION: Plan deepened with 6 parallel research agents. Key improvements: (1) Security — magic byte validation + Pillow re-encoding required for uploads; storage_path must NOT be static-mounted, serve via authenticated GET endpoint; cross-org IDOR guard needed on doctor logo endpoint; legacy RoleChecker must be replaced with get_current_user_with_rls. (2) Architecture — get_logo_path() moved to utils/logo_utils.py (pure function, no DB); logo_path stored as relative path in DB; add storage_path_logos to settings.py; atomic writes via os.replace(). (3) Frontend — URL.createObjectURL for preview; native HTML5 drag-drop; canvas resize for oversized images; role={undefined} on MUI Button component=label. (4) Migration — confirmed revision ID n2c3d4e5f6a7 correct, next is o3d4e5f6a7b8; no RLS policy changes needed; PostgreSQL-only (no SQLite compat). (5) Archive — git tag preferred over branch; audit cross-imports before deleting.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Applied plan review feedback. 20 recommendations across beads .3, .4, .5. Key changes: (1) Pillow re-encode replaces magic byte validation, thumbnail(800,800) server-side, Pillow in sync endpoint; (2) os.path.realpath()+os.sep for path containment; (3) rate limiting, audit logging, Cache-Control on GET logo added to .3; (4) generate_recibo_pdf noted as different arch but user decision: redesign to ReportLab flowables; (5) frontend simplified — removed canvas resize, drag-drop, LinearProgress.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: pathlib.Path used for all path operations. Path.resolve() replaces os.path.realpath(), Path.relative_to() is the canonical containment check (raises ValueError, no string manipulation). Path.unlink(missing_ok=True) replaces try/except FileNotFoundError. Project requires Python >=3.11 so all patterns are fully available.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: generate_recibo_pdf will be redesigned from PyPDF2 canvas overlay to ReportLab flowables (SimpleDocTemplate + platypus), matching the architecture of all other PDF functions. The canvas overlay approach was an old decision being dropped. All 5 PDF functions now use the same pattern.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: generate_recibo_pdf redesign moved to medsimples-ycer.6. Bead .4 covers the other 4 PDF functions only: generate_visit_summary_pdf, generate_prescription_pdf, generate_issued_invoices_pdf, generate_unpaid_invoices_pdf. The recibo function's PyPDF2 canvas overlay is left untouched by .4.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: pdf_templates had cross-dependencies in schemas/__init__.py (__all__ + imports), settings.py (storage_path_pdf_templates property), models.py (3 model classes), and main.py (import + include_router). All cleaned up. Archive tag pushed as archive/pdf-templates before deletion.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Migration o3d4e5f6a7b8 blocked by pre-existing failure in m1b2c3d4e5f6 (invites table). That migration runs CREATE TYPE invitestatus in a DO block but the dev DB at 10.10.0.47:5432 is read-only (likely hot standby). Fix: run migrations against primary write node, or stamp to n2c3d4e5f6a7 if invites migration was already applied manually on prod.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Logo upload pipeline = fast-reject on size -> MIME gate -> bounded read -> Pillow decode+load -> thumbnail(800,800) -> convert('RGB').save PNG -> atomic write via tempfile+os.replace -> old file cleanup after new write succeeds. Both org and doctor services follow identical pattern. GET endpoints use FileResponse with Cache-Control: private, no-cache, must-revalidate. Doctor endpoint does cross-org IDOR guard via organization_id filter returning 404, not 403.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: get_logo_path(doctor, org) resolves logo chain (doctor > org > static fallback) using Path.resolve() + relative_to() for path-traversal protection. Returns None when no logo exists at all. PDF generators guard with 'if logo_path and os.path.exists(logo_path)'.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: LogoUpload uses URL.createObjectURL for preview but immediately revokes after successful upload — avoids memory leak while still showing the candidate image. Cache-bust after upload via ?t=Date.now() forces img reload without needing a state refresh of the full entity.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Replacing a canvas.Canvas overlay approach with SimpleDocTemplate flowables requires rethinking the layout from absolute coordinates to a top-to-bottom story. The key difference: canvas draws at fixed (x, y) positions while flowables are stacked sequentially — you must restructure the content as label/value pairs in Tables rather than hardcoded coordinate offsets.
