Also: unused ParagraphStyle variables that are defined but never referenced will cause ruff F841 errors, so define only what is consumed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: multipart/form-data Content-Type must never be set manually when using FormData with Axios — doing so removes the boundary and causes 400 on the server. Always let Axios set it automatically.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Use uploadingRef = useRef(false) as a synchronous guard against concurrent uploads in React. disabled state alone is insufficient because React batches state updates between render cycles.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Removed logo_path from both update schemas — logo management is exclusively via dedicated upload/delete endpoints to prevent path injection

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: _validate_tax_id and _validate_phone_number were dead methods in doctor_service.py — removed. Neither was called anywhere in the backend. Validation for doctor data (tax ID, phone) is now handled upstream by Pydantic schemas (DoctorUpdate) and/or the frontend.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: useEffect exhaustive-deps fix — inline async function inside useEffect or use useCallback; never omit referenced functions from deps array

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS is a global class attribute on Pillow's Image module — must be set at module load time, not per-request, to avoid race conditions in concurrent server requests

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: ProfileSettings.tsx DoctorData interface declared both logo_url and logo_path fields, but /api/users/me returns DoctorInfo schema which only has logo_path. The LogoUpload component receives currentLogoPath and constructs the display URL internally. Removed the spurious logo_url field from DoctorData interface; logo_path was already correctly passed to LogoUpload at line 555.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Raised frontend logo upload limit to 5MB to match backend — backend is the authoritative security gate, frontend limit is UX feedback only

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Font registration guard — module-level _fonts_registered = False flag prevents per-PDF TTF re-reads. Check before registerFont calls, set True after. Both medical_service.py and invoice_service.py now use this pattern. The guard short-circuits _register_pdf_fonts() returning immediately if already registered.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: GET logo endpoint was missing org-isolation guard — added same check as POST/DELETE: superadmin or same-org required

author: Roberto Mello
text: FIX: _to_response now passes logo_path=org.logo_path from org model — was silently dropped, causing always-None in API responses. OrganizationResponse.logo_path is str | None; no logo_url computed field exists, so the DB field is exposed directly.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Relative path for logo storage now derived from settings.storage_path_logos relative to settings.storage_path — no hardcoded 'logos' substring

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Replaced HTTPException in service layer with NotFoundError from utils/exceptions.py — routers translate via exception handler

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Moved inline db.query(Doctor) from router to DoctorService.get_doctor_by_id_in_org() — IDOR guard now encapsulated in service layer

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: DoctorResponse schema in doctor.py had a model_validator(mode='before') that hardcoded the route string /api/doctors/{id}/logo to construct logo_url from logo_path. Removed the validator; logo_url is now a plain str|None=None field. Added DoctorService._to_response() which sets logo_url=f'/api/doctors/{doctor.id}/logo' if doctor.logo_path else None. Added _get_doctor_raw() private method for internal ORM access so methods like upload_logo/delete_logo/get_logo_path still work with raw Doctor objects. OrganizationService._to_response() was already clean (linter had already added logo_path=org.logo_path). No hardcoded API routes remain in any Pydantic schema. Ruff passes.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Fixed path traversal guard ordering in both upload_logo methods — guard now runs before mkdir/write. Order: compute dest → resolve().relative_to(base) → mkdir → write

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: slowapi is fully configured in the project (middleware/rate_limiter.py + app.state.limiter in main.py). The limiter uses get_real_ip() for correct IP extraction behind proxies. GET logo endpoints got 30/minute, DELETE logo endpoints got 5/minute — matching the pattern used by the existing POST logo endpoints.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Extracted save_logo_file(content, dest, old_path, storage_path) to utils/logo_utils.py — shared by org and doctor upload pipelines. Handles: Pillow decode+load validation, thumbnail(800,800)+convert('RGB') PNG encode, atomic write via tempfile+os.replace, old file unlink after successful write. Callers generate the dest path and relative_path themselves; save_logo_file is purely I/O.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Never set Content-Type manually for FormData/Axios uploads — boundary is stripped, causing 400. Let Axios auto-set it.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: uploadingRef = useRef(false) synchronous guard prevents concurrent upload race — set true at start of handleFileChange, cleared in finally. Disabled prop alone insufficient.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Object URL lifecycle managed via ref — revoke-before-replace + unmount cleanup. All other revokeObjectURL calls removed to prevent double-revocation.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: useState(\!\!prop) initializes from prop but doesn't sync on prop updates — add useEffect([prop]) to keep state in sync with external prop changes

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: This bead was already resolved by .21. ProfileSettings.tsx and OrganizationDetail.tsx do not track logo presence with their own local hasLogo state — they pass currentLogoPath directly to <LogoUpload> as a prop. The useEffect([currentLogoPath]) sync fix in LogoUpload.tsx (lines 66-68) is the only location where the fix was needed. The pre-existing tsc error in InvoiceReports.tsx (unrelated) was present before this bead.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: GET /organizations/{id} had no org-isolation auth check — any authenticated user could read any org's CNPJ, contacts, and filesystem logo_path by incrementing the ID.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: OrganizationResponse exposes raw filesystem relative path (logo_path) while DoctorResponse correctly uses computed logo_url — inconsistency is a latent path-disclosure risk if storage ever goes static.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: _fonts_registered module-level bool in medical_service and invoice_service is not thread-safe — needs threading.Lock double-checked locking to prevent concurrent PDF requests from double-registering fonts.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: LogoUpload.tsx needs mountedRef = useRef(true) guard in all async operations — upload and remove both need concurrent-operation guards (uploadingRef AND removingRef) to prevent race between the two operations.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Switched GET /organizations/{id} from get_current_user to get_current_user_with_rls and added org-isolation guard before service call. Non-superadmin users requesting an org_id that doesn't match their own now receive 403. Consistent with the pattern already used by GET /organizations/{id}/logo.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced logo_path with logo_url in OrganizationResponse. logo_url is set to /api/organizations/{id}/logo in _to_response() when org.logo_path is set, matching the DoctorService pattern. Frontend OrganizationDetail.tsx (not in file ownership) must be updated to rename logo_path to logo_url in its Organization interface and the currentLogoPath prop at line 330. LogoUpload only uses currentLogoPath as a boolean (!!currentLogoPath), so logo_url is a safe drop-in replacement.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Double-checked locking for thread-safe one-time initialization — outer check avoids lock contention on the hot path; inner check prevents race after lock acquisition. Use threading.Lock() at module level alongside the flag, keeping _fonts_registered = True inside the with block after all side effects complete.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added Doctor.active.is_(True) filter to get_by_id_in_org() in the repository layer. This ensures logo upload/get/delete endpoints return 404 for inactive (soft-deleted/anonymized) doctors. The LGPD export endpoint uses a different query path and is unaffected. Filter placed in repository per layered architecture conventions.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Use a cancelled boolean flag in useEffect cleanup to guard all setState calls (setOrganization, setError, setLoading) against setState-after-unmount. Return () => { cancelled = true; } as the cleanup function. This is simpler than AbortController when using promise-based fetch utilities like cachedAxiosGet that don't accept a signal.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: mountedRef guard for async setState — add mountedRef = useRef(true), set false in cleanup useEffect, guard all post-await setState calls with 'if (\!mountedRef.current) return/skip'. Reset input value synchronously (before await) to avoid touching detached DOM node in finally. uploadingRef.current = false always runs; only setUploading(false) needs the guard. Merge objectUrl cleanup into the single cleanup useEffect (set ref to null after revoking).

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Replaced bare RuntimeError('Storage path error') with ValidationError('Requisição inválida') in logo_utils.py (save_logo_file), organization_service.py (get_logo_path), and doctor_service.py (get_logo_path). RuntimeError has no FastAPI handler and leaks a 500 with traceback; ValidationError subclasses HTTPException and maps to HTTP 400. No new imports needed — all three files already imported ValidationError.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: replaced manual chunk-read loops (file.file.read in sync loop) with await file.read() in both upload_org_logo and upload_doctor_logo. Also converted both endpoints from def to async def. The chunk loop was necessary for streaming multi-GB uploads; for logo uploads capped at 5 MB it just adds complexity. The size guard moved to after file.read() using len(content) since file.size is not always populated for all clients.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Added inline comment on logo_url line in DoctorService._to_response() documenting the coupling to routers/doctors.py prefix. Chose comment over constant extraction per bead guidance — the route prefix is stable and over-engineering with a constant import would add complexity without benefit.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: For async handlers in React, pair each useRef(false) guard (e.g. removingRef) with an uploadingRef cross-check so upload+remove races are blocked in both directions. The ref guard fires synchronously before the first await, unlike disabled state which only takes effect after a re-render cycle. Also wrap all post-await state updates with mountedRef.current checks to prevent setState-on-unmounted-component warnings.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: doctor_service.py had one .dict() call at line 64 in update_doctor_profile — update_data.dict(exclude_unset=True) replaced with update_data.model_dump(exclude_unset=True). No other .dict() calls existed in the file. Pydantic v2 deprecates .dict() in favor of .model_dump(); both work but ruff/mypy will warn on .dict() in strict configs.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: DoctorResponse has only 3 fields (id: int required, name: str required, logo_url: str | None = None optional). BaseResponse has no required fields. _to_response() correctly populates all required fields — no Pydantic validation error is possible. Frontend Doctor interfaces only need id and name (used in dropdowns); logo_url is not accessed from DoctorResponse by any frontend consumer. The bead's premise of a silent regression is confirmed NOT to exist — the schema contract is fulfilled. No code changes required.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Magic-byte sniffing before Pillow decode: _sniff_mime() checks raw bytes against known image signatures (PNG \x89PNG, JPEG \xff\xd8\xff, GIF GIF8, WebP RIFF...WEBP) and raises ValidationError if unrecognised. This runs before Image.open() so a client cannot bypass the allowlist by spoofing Content-Type. The HTTP multipart content_type header is untrusted — never use it as the sole format guard.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: To prevent post-upload image flicker, don't clear the preview URL immediately on upload success. Instead, keep previewUrl alive and clear it only in the img onLoad callback — this guarantees the preview stays visible until the server image is confirmed loaded. setHasLogo(true) + setLogoTimestamp() are still set immediately so displaySrc switches to the server URL src, but the visible image never disappears because the browser reuses the already-rendered element and fires onLoad when the new src finishes loading.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS fires after decompression begins; the decompression bomb guard must use img.width * img.height immediately after Image.open() (header-only) and before img.load(). The try/except was split into two blocks so the dimension check sits between open and load, outside any except clause.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Made storage_path a required keyword-only param using * separator (def save_logo_file(content, dest, *, old_path=None, storage_path: Path)). Removed Optional import, removed both 'if storage_path is not None:' guards — path traversal check and old-file cleanup now always run. Both callers in organization_service.py and doctor_service.py already passed storage_path as a keyword arg, so no caller changes needed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: structlog ignores Python logging-style extra={} dicts — fields end up nested under 'extra' key in log output instead of top-level. Always use direct kwargs: logger.info('event', field=value). Also: inline re-imports inside function bodies (e.g. from ..utils.exceptions import X as Y) signal incomplete refactors — remove them and use the top-level import.

author: Roberto Mello
text: DECISION: Created medsimples/backend/app/utils/pdf_fonts.py with a single ensure_fonts_registered() function using one module-level _registered flag + threading.Lock (double-checked locking). Both medical_service.py and invoice_service.py now import and call ensure_fonts_registered() instead of maintaining their own _fonts_registered/_font_lock globals and _register_pdf_fonts() static methods. Removed pdfmetrics/TTFont/threading imports from both services. The invoice service previously returned a tuple (font_name, font_bold) — callers updated to derive font_bold from the returned font_name string, matching the medical_service pattern.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: download_invoice_pdf had no auth dependency (only get_db), so RLS context was never set. PostgreSQL RLS filtered out all rows, causing invoice_not_found 404 even after successful PDF generation. Symptom: 404 in 7ms — too fast for any file I/O, meaning the DB query returned nothing.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Every endpoint that queries RLS-protected tables MUST have require_financial_access() or get_current_user_with_rls as a dependency, even read-only download endpoints. Missing auth = invisible data.

author: Roberto Mello
text: FACT: logo_utils.py used a lazy import 'from ..core.settings import settings' inside get_logo_path(). settings.py lives at app/settings.py (not app/core/settings.py). Ruff does not catch wrong relative import paths — only pyright/mypy would. This is why medsimples-1qty (add pyright) was filed.

author: Roberto Mello
text: LEARNED: Lazy imports inside function bodies hide import errors until that code path is executed in production. Prefer top-level imports so errors surface at startup.

author: Roberto Mello
text: PATTERN: Never use hardcoded hex colors in sx props for themed components. Use MUI theme tokens (bgcolor: action.hover, borderColor: divider, bgcolor: background.paper) so dark mode works automatically. Hardcoded #RRGGBB always breaks in dark mode.

author: Roberto Mello
text: BUG: window.open() cannot send Authorization headers — gets 401 on JWT-protected PDF endpoints. Two cases found and fixed in InvoiceList.tsx and InvoiceDetail.tsx. FIX: use axios with responseType blob and Authorization header, then createObjectURL + programmatic link click. AVOID: Never use window.open or bare anchor href for /api/ endpoints requiring JWT auth.
